what_it_does: The codified design-language document for the ConceptV Design System — the cross-cutting rules every screen obeys, the intent behind the tokens. It states ~18 numbered laws covering multi-surface authoring, responsive/computed sizing, choreographed motion, visual-first design, depth/texture, colour discipline (two saturated voices), theme modes, density, composition primitives, loading-as-resolve, authorship legibility, exports-as-surfaces, the one-home anti-drift law, AI-as-registry, the CV_HOST repo bridge, narrative title-chain architecture, universal components (slots/sockets), and the Axes registry.
observations: Section numbering is irregular (jumps 16 then 18 then 17 at the end) suggesting append-as-discovered growth.
Section 1: three canonical surfaces (desktop 1440x900, mobile portrait 390x844, mobile landscape 844x390, optional tablet), 'author once present many', 44px touch-min, safe-area insets.
Section 2 bans content-only sizing: fluid type clamps, .min0 + .truncate/.line-clamp, .pill-group, .auto-grid, .measure.
Section 3 motion: enter/exit/move via View Transitions or FLIP, .stagger waves, gated by prefers-reduced-motion.

Section 6 colour discipline: exactly two saturated voices — gold (--accent-gold/--vi-* = active/decision) and sage-green (--accent-communication/--comm-* = communication/relationship), structure is bronze, success is a state not a voice; clean<->warm ground knob (data-ground).
Section 9 composition: layouts are content-as-data, pure function content->CVNode tree via Slide/Archetypes and the block solver; new atom is a registered renderer (ContainmentTree.registerAtom).
Section 13 the anti-drift law: four single-source registries (tokens, types via CV_REGISTRY, engine solvers, AI via CV_AI), one home per concept.
Section 14 AI-as-registry: providers/behaviours/skills/capabilities/context; ai = f(capability, context, params) mirroring design = f(content, axis); 43 capabilities across 14 families.

Section 15 the Bridge CV_HOST: fifth registry, runtimes review/fsapi/native, a serializer turning mutations into exact source, repo.*/ds.propose/ds.apply as CV_AI capabilities, sandbox-review staging.

Section 16 narrative title-chain: slide titles chain into one running sentence via leading connectives, deck Type carries titleChain intent, section breaks full-bleed photo dividers, cover/closing are the live product surface.
Section 18 universal components: Type declares parts/value-slots/sockets/conditions via CV_COND; candidatesForSocket; interface is a projection; 94 Types live.
Section 17 (last) Axes: sixth registry CV_AXES (Colour/Space/Size/Motion/Texture/Depth/Fill/Form/Symbol + Meaning over them); tokens ARE the axis value-units; components subscribe to axes.
standouts: design = f(content, axis), ai = f(capability, context, params), capability-available = f(environment, runtime) — the same generative shape applied across six registries.
The one-home anti-drift law as the central organizing principle binding tokens/types/engine/AI/host/axes.
The corpus-proven narrative title-chain rule (titles parse as one paragraph: problem->thesis->mechanism->proof->moat->ask).
Exactly two saturated voices (gold, sage-green) with bronze as structure — a strict semantic colour discipline.
conventions: Self-describing doc kept in lockstep with code (CLAUDE.md mandates DESIGN-LANGUAGE.md updated when a rule is added).
No-versioning/grows-in-place style; sections appended as principles are discovered.
Every rule pointed at its token/registry home and its spec/analysis companion doc.
No ranking language; principles named, not ordered.
concerns: Section numbering is out of order (16, 18, 17 at the end) which can mislead a reader scanning by number.
States 'this layer — done' for foundation tokens and '43 capabilities'/'94 Types' as point-in-time counts that can drift from the live registries.
notes: The intent-behind-the-tokens charter: ~18 cross-cutting laws making the whole system one generative, single-sourced, multi-surface, anti-drift design language across six registries.
questions: Why does the section numbering jump (16 then 18 then 17) — is the order significant or an artifact of growth?
Are the stated counts (43 capabilities, 94 Types, 'done' foundation layer) kept in sync with the live registries or snapshots?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the doc states it is the how-we-design intent behind the tokens and delivers exactly that as a set of binding cross-cutting laws.
apparent_themes: Generative f(content, axis) shape repeated across registries
Self-describing system kept in lockstep with code
intention_signals: Codify the entire ConceptV design philosophy as a coherent, single-sourced, generative system of registries so that every screen, deck, and AI move is computed from one home and nothing drifts — and so a fresh agent inherits the intent, not just the tokens.
novelty: The unification of six same-shaped registries (tokens, types, engine, AI, host bridge, axes) under one generative function form, plus the corpus-proven narrative title-chain law and the two-voice colour discipline.
contribution: Contributes the authoritative statement of the design-language laws and the registry/axis vocabulary that a fused system would keep as its design intent and anti-drift contract.
summary_for_embedding: A design-language markdown codifying ~18 cross-cutting ConceptV rules — multi-surface authoring, computed layout, choreographed motion, two-voice colour, six single-source registries (tokens/types/engine/AI/host/axes), narrative title-chains, and universal slot/socket components.
intention_for_embedding: To codify the intent behind the tokens as a single generative, single-sourced, anti-drift design system so every surface and AI move is computed from one home.
